draw_wan_waypoints.description: Draw interfaces that terminate on a circuit / provider network as a link to a gray WAN cloud waypoint (one per far-end name). When false, those interfaces are skipped (reported).

synthesize_host_stubs.description: Give an uncabled port that carries a VLAN / IP a synthetic 'dummy_stub_N' peer (interface 'Dummy <n-1>') so the port -- and its VLAN/IP -- exists in NS. One stub per device; forced light-gray to flag it as inferred. When false, such VLANs/IPs are skipped (reported) instead.

include_unconnected.description: Keep devices that have no cabled L1 link. Default false is offline-faithful (only connected devices are placed). When true, uncabled devices are shown in a dedicated 'unlinked' area (they still carry no ports/L2/L3, since NS has no standalone-port command).

color_overrides.description: Map of NS device name -> [R,G,B] to override a device's role-based Default-cell colour. By default real devices are role-coloured by the shared palette (green network gear / red server / yellow client), observed WAN/provider WayPoints (e.g. Level3 MPLS) are NS's native light blue [220,230,242], and only synthetic dummy_stub_N peers are gray; use this only to recolour specific devices.
